Home / Common Pests
Know Your Enemy

Common Pests in
Upstate New York

Upstate NY's wooded terrain, harsh winters, older housing stock, and seasonal swings create ideal conditions for a wide range of pests. Know what you're dealing with — then call Sasquatch.

Seasonal Pest Activity

What's Active When

Spring
March – May
  • Carpenter ants emerging
  • Termite swarm season
  • Tick season begins
  • Stink bugs leaving walls
  • Wasps building new nests
  • Mosquitoes hatching
Summer
June – August
  • Mosquitoes peak
  • Yellowjackets aggressive
  • Deer ticks active
  • Ants foraging heavily
  • Spotted lanternfly active
  • Wildlife most mobile
Fall
Sept – November
  • Stink bugs invading homes
  • Mice seeking warmth
  • Cluster flies in walls
  • Boxelder bugs swarming
  • Yellowjackets desperate
  • Raccoons fattening up
Winter
Dec – February
  • Mice & rats indoors
  • Cockroaches in warmth
  • Stink bugs in walls
  • Cluster flies dormant
  • Squirrels in attics
  • Bed bugs year-round
Pest Profiles

Upstate NY's Most Problematic Pests

Spring–Fall
🐜Carpenter Ant

Upstate NY's #1 structural pest. Large black ants that excavate wood to build galleries — unlike termites, they don't eat it. Found in moisture-damaged wood in walls, decks, and window frames. Colonies can grow to thousands of workers.

High — Structural
April – September
Sawdust piles, large ants
Walls, decks, logs
Get Help With This Pest →
Year-Round
🐭White-Footed Mouse

The dominant mouse in Upstate NY's wooded and suburban areas. Deer-tan colored with white belly. A known Lyme disease reservoir via ticks — making them a serious health risk. They enter homes in fall through gaps as small as a dime.

High — Health Risk
Fall – Winter
Droppings, gnaw marks
Walls, attics, kitchens
Get Help With This Pest →
Spring–Fall
🕷️Deer Tick (Black-Legged)

The primary Lyme disease vector in the Northeast — and Upstate NY has some of the highest Lyme rates in the country. Tiny sesame-seed-sized ticks active from early spring through late fall, even on warm winter days.

Extreme — Lyme Disease
May – October
Found on skin/clothing
Yard edges, tall grass
Get Help With This Pest →
Fall Invader
🪲Brown Marmorated Stink Bug

An invasive species that's taken over Upstate NY. Starting late summer, massive numbers congregate on warm south-facing walls before invading attics and walls to overwinter. Crushing them releases a distinctive foul odor.

Medium — Nuisance
August – November
Masses on walls, odor
Walls, attics, windows
Get Help With This Pest →
Year-Round
🦝Raccoon

Highly intelligent and bold, raccoons regularly tear through roof vents to access attics, causing thousands in structural damage. They carry raccoon roundworm and rabies. A nuisance wildlife problem that needs professional removal.

High — Disease & Damage
Spring (nesting)
Noise at night, damage
Attics, chimneys, decks
Get Help With This Pest →
Late Summer
🐝Yellowjacket & Bald-Faced Hornet

The most aggressive stinging insects in Upstate NY. Yellowjackets nest underground and in walls. Bald-faced hornets build papery aerial nests. Both become extremely aggressive in late summer when colonies peak.

High — Sting Risk
July – September
Nest, heavy activity
Ground, eaves, trees
Get Help With This Pest →
Invasive Species
🪲Spotted Lanternfly

An invasive planthopper rapidly spreading through New York State, first detected in NY in 2020. It feeds on sap from over 70 plant species including grapevines and fruit trees, excreting honeydew that promotes sooty mold.

High — Agricultural
July – November
Masses on trees, sticky residue
Trees, outdoor structures
Get Help With This Pest →
Year-Round Risk
🏠Subterranean Termite

Silent destroyers that go undetected for years while causing catastrophic structural damage. Upstate NY's older housing stock is especially vulnerable. They build mud tubes and can consume a 2x4 stud in several months.

Extreme — Structural
March – May (swarms)
Mud tubes, swarmers
Foundation, sill plates
Get Help With This Pest →
Fall Invader
🪰Cluster Fly

A hallmark nuisance pest of rural Upstate NY. In fall, thousands of large, sluggish flies congregate on sun-warmed exterior walls and push into wall voids and attics to overwinter. On warm days, they emerge indoors in enormous numbers.

Medium — Nuisance
September – October
Masses on south walls
Attics, wall voids
Get Help With This Pest →

Spotted One of These?

Don't wait for a small pest problem to become a major infestation. Call Sasquatch for a FREE inspection — we'll identify the pest and tell you exactly what needs to be done.
